text.tspan: OAUTH 2.0 + OPENID CONNECT (OIDC) authorization-code flow with PKCE
the modern standard for federated auth on web and mobile
1. USER clicks 'Sign in with Google' in your app
2. APP generates code_verifier (random) + code_challenge = SHA256(verifier)
3. APP redirects browser to IdP authorize endpoint:
https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth?
client_id=YOUR_APP_ID
redirect_uri=https://yourapp.com/callback
response_type=code
scope=openid email profile
code_challenge=...
state=... (CSRF protection)
4. IDP authenticates user (password + MFA + risk checks)
5. IDP asks user to consent (if first time): 'allow YourApp to access your email?'
6. IDP redirects browser BACK to your app's callback with an authorization code:
https://yourapp.com/callback?code=AUTH_CODE&state=...
(the auth code is short-lived, ~1 min, single-use, useless without verifier)
7. APP backend exchanges code for tokens (server-to-server, NOT in browser):
POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
grant_type=authorization_code
code=AUTH_CODE
code_verifier=... (proves we are the one that started the flow)
client_id=YOUR_APP_ID + client_secret
redirect_uri=https://yourapp.com/callback
Response:
id_token (OIDC: signed JWT proving user identity)
access_token (OAuth 2.0: lets you call APIs as user)
refresh_token (long-lived, get new access tokens)
8. APP verifies id_token signature against IdP's public key (JWKS)
9. APP creates its own session for the user (cookie / app token)
10. User is logged in.
Later: if access_token expires, use refresh_token to get a new one
(without re-prompting the user)
KEY PROPERTIES
u2022 password NEVER touches your app u2014 only the IdP sees it
u2022 authorization code is exchanged server-side, not in the browser
u2022 PKCE protects against code interception (mandatory for mobile, recommended for web SPAs)
u2022 state parameter prevents CSRF on the callback
u2022 id_token = OIDC authentication; access_token = OAuth 2.0 API authorization
u2022 OAuth 2.0 alone is authorization; OIDC adds authentication on top
IMPLICIT FLOW IS DEPRECATED (token in URL was bad). Authorization code + PKCE only.
